metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit `depends_on: 457a` to child 94fd to enforce that the manifest assembler is not started before `delivered_now` exists.
findings.finding: Child 94fd implicitly depends on 457a (calls `delivered_now` in the manifest assembler) but declares no explicit `depends_on` on 457a; if 94fd is picked up before 457a is delivered, the manifest assembler cannot be implemented as specified.
findings.suggested_fix: Add `depends_on: [457a-e8da-92c4-450e]` to 94fd's metadata, and note in the scope that the manifest assembler can only be completed after 457a is merged.

findings.finding: Acceptance-criteria / definition-of-done quality issues.
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify in the ADR (AC 6) and AC 4 whether a parent epic reopen (ADR 0009 event) cascades to invalidate children's completion attestations, or whether children's delivered-now status is evaluated independently of the parent's attestation lifecycle.
findings.finding: The Context section states 'ADR 0009 invalidates an attestation signed before the reopen' as a re-fire trigger, but neither AC 4 (delivered-now predicate) nor the ADR deliverable (AC 6) specifies whether a parent epic reopen cascades to invalidate children's completion attestations. An implementer must decide: do children remain 'delivered-now' after a parent reopen (suppression still applies to them), or does ADR 0009 cascade to un-deliver them (all findings resurface)?
findings.location: Context section ('ADR 0009 invalidates an attestation signed before the reopen') vs AC item 4 ('valid completion attestation … reusing the `_child_closure_findings` predicate')
findings.suggested_fix: Add a single sentence to AC 4: 'A parent reopen event (ADR 0009) does not invalidate children's completion attestations; each child's delivered-now status is evaluated independently on the child's own attestation validity.' Then reflect this in the ADR schema.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify that AC4's predicate reuse means applying `compute_validity(sig, child_state, 'completion-verifier')` (the inner call inside `_child_closure_findings`) to identify DELIVERED children — not calling `_child_closure_findings` itself, which returns a non-delivery finding list (the complement set).
findings.finding: The `delivered-now` predicate says it reuses 'the `_child_closure_findings` predicate', but in the codebase `_child_closure_findings` is a deficit-detector: it returns FAILURE findings for each child that LACKS a valid completion attestation — the inverse of the delivery confirmation needed. An implementer who literally reuses this function gets a list of non-delivered children, not delivered ones. The plan needs to clarify that what is reused is the `compute_validity(sig, child_state, 'completion-verifier')` call inside `_child_closure_findings`, applied to children whose status IS 'closed', to identify the delivered-now set.
findings.location: AC4: '`delivered-now` = valid completion attestation (validity-on-read, reusing the `_child_closure_findings` predicate)'
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'reusing the `_child_closure_findings` predicate' with 'reusing the `compute_validity(sig, child_state, "completion-verifier")` inner call from `_child_closure_findings`, returning True only when a closed child has a certified, currently-valid completion-verifier attestation'.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Make the calibration gold-set criterion measurable: specify a minimum count and the coverage dimensions required (e.g., '≥6 cases covering: security finding on delivered child, contract finding on delivered child, cross-sibling finding, force-closed child finding, in-progress child finding, and one must-suppress case as a control').
findings.finding: The 'calibration gold-set of must-never-suppress cases' in AC7 is effort-framed and size-unspecified: no count, no domain coverage requirement, and no definition of what constitutes a 'complete' gold-set is given, making it impossible to evaluate in-session whether the AC is satisfied.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'a calibration gold-set of must-never-suppress cases' with 'a calibration gold-set of ≥5 labeled cases, one per must-never-suppress category (security, contract, cross-sibling span, force-closed child, in-progress child), plus ≥1 labeled must-suppress control case, stored as fixture files under tests/fixtures/completion_gold_set/.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the 'superseded by a sibling' predicate concretely: specify what field, relationship type, or signed artifact on a child ticket signals supersession (e.g., a `supersedes` link type, a structured close-reason enum value, or an explicit note in the sibling's completion attestation).
findings.finding: The 'superseded by a sibling under the same parent' half of the delivered-now predicate is never concretely defined: no field, relationship type, content-match rule, or data source is specified. An executor cannot implement AC4 without stopping to decide what 'supersede' means.
findings.location: ## Acceptance Criteria — AC4; ## What we build (delivered-now predicate); ## Scope (delivered-now predicate)
findings.suggested_fix: Add a subsection under the delivered-now predicate: 'A child C is superseded by sibling S iff S carries a signed completion attestation referencing C's ID in a `supersedes` field (same parent, same epoch).' This reuses the attestation mechanism already in scope and requires no new attestation kind.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Either remove AC item 1 (the decision record is already covered by the ADR deliverable in AC 6, which 'codifies the new contracts') or convert it to a verifiable forward-pointing criterion such as 'The ADR references the alternatives-considered rationale with a durable link to the session log.'
findings.finding: AC item 1 is pre-marked 'DONE' and its verify step points to artifacts that are the plan document itself ('decision comment + the Alternatives considered section + the completion session log'). This makes it a self-referential criterion that measures a completed artifact rather than a future deliverable, conflating the planning record with an acceptance gate and creating ambiguity for reviewers about what remains to be shipped.
findings.location: AC item 1: 'Brainstorm + decision recorded (a vs b vs hybrid) — DONE'
findings.suggested_fix: Remove AC item 1 and add a sub-bullet to AC 6 requiring the ADR to include a 'Alternatives Considered' section that captures the a/b/hybrid decision rationale — consolidating the decision record into the durable ADR artifact.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit rollback/killswitch section naming the evidence-gate flag, its off-state semantics (floor disabled → pre-floor behavior restored), and the procedure to disable the completion floor if it misfires in production.
findings.finding: The evidence-gate flag (mentioned in AC bullet 3 as 'gated behind an evidence-gate flag') is never explicitly framed as the rollback/killswitch for the completion floor, and its off-state behavior is not described. T4 requires an explicit rollback/back-out plan — acknowledgement of the flag's existence is insufficient.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — bullet 3: 'gated behind an evidence-gate flag + configurable priority floor'
findings.suggested_fix: In the ADR (docs/adr/0020-…) and in the AC, add: 'Setting evidence-gate=false disables the completion floor entirely, restoring pre-0020 Pass-3 output; this is the rollback path if the floor misfires. The flag name, default value, and override mechanism are [X].'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify that ADR 0008 and ADR 0002 will be amended (or cross-referenced via a 'See also: ADR 0020') to note that Pass-3 now has three floors, so readers of those ADRs are not left with an incomplete picture of suppression logic.
findings.finding: ADR 0008 (novelty floor) and ADR 0002 (drift-refresh path) describe Pass-3's floor/path machinery but will not be updated to reference the new third floor introduced by ADR 0020, leaving them as incomplete — and potentially misleading — standalone references to Pass-3 behavior.
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the ADR 0020 scope and to the plan's Acceptance Criteria: 'ADR 0008 and ADR 0002 receive a "See also: ADR 0020 — completion floor" cross-reference note so Pass-3's full suppression surface is navigable from any of the three floor ADRs.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Make the criterion exclusion set (currently `{security, contract}`) configurable (e.g., a named constant or config entry alongside the priority floor and evidence-gate flag) so that always-preserve criteria can be extended without code changes.
findings.finding: The criterion exclusion set `{security, contract}` in the Pass-3 completion-floor drop condition is hardcoded, yet it is a business rule that is likely to evolve (e.g., adding G6, T2, or other always-preserve criteria as the system matures), while the adjacent priority floor and evidence-gate flag are explicitly made configurable.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC line 3 (Pass-3 completion floor drop condition)
findings.suggested_fix: Add a `COMPLETION_FLOOR_ALWAYS_PRESERVE_CRITERIA` config entry (defaulting to `[security, contract]`) alongside the existing `evidence_gate` flag and `priority_floor`, and reference it in the ADR schema; this mirrors the pattern already used for the priority floor.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] In the child breakdown, sequence the Pass-2 sub-call (+ its calibration gold-set) as an evidence-gated first child so its {attribution, containment, layer} schema is validated before Pass-3 floor and sidecar children are built on top of it.
findings.finding: The epic lists all 7 acceptance criteria as a flat batch with no stated child-ordering that de-risks the riskiest piece first. The highest-risk technical question — whether the Pass-2 sub-call reliably classifies {attribution, containment, layer} — is not visibly validated before Pass-3 and the sidecar are built on top of it.
findings.location: ## Acceptance Criteria (all 7 items) and ## What we build

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the sibling-supersede arm of delivered-now precisely: name the direction of the `supersedes` relation that qualifies, and require the superseding sibling itself to satisfy the attestation-based delivered-now condition (i.e., a delivered-now child supersedes child X → X is delivered-now; not merely any sibling holding a link).
findings.finding: The 'in-epic sibling supersede' arm of the delivered-now predicate is mechanistically underspecified in two ways that can produce incorrect delivered-now decisions: (1) the plan does not state which direction of the `supersedes` relation qualifies (in the codebase `supersedes` is directional and non-reciprocal per `src/rebar/graph/_links.py:22`); (2) the plan does not require the superseding sibling itself to carry a valid delivered-now attestation. A bare `supersedes` link from any sibling — including an open or force-closed one — could satisfy the predicate as written.
findings.location: AC4: 'OR superseded by a sibling under the same parent'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the data-flow between the Pass-2 completion sub-call and the delivered-now manifest: state explicitly whether the sub-call receives the delivered-now set as context (and ATTRIBUTION is a closed-child name the sub-call emits), and that Pass-3 then does the final delivered-now membership check — mirroring how the novelty sub-call receives prior findings as injected context.
findings.finding: The data-flow between the Pass-2 `plan_review_completion` sub-call and the delivered-now manifest is not specified. The ATTRIBUTION sub-question asks which closed child a finding belongs to, but the Pass-3 completion floor then needs to check whether that attributed child is in the delivered-now set. The plan is silent on whether (a) the sub-call receives the delivered-now manifest as context so it can self-filter, (b) the sub-call receives all closed children and Pass-3 does the delivered-now lookup, or (c) the sub-call is called separately per child. This ordering ambiguity matters: if ATTRIBUTION is answered by the LLM without seeing the delivered-now manifest, Pass-3 must do the delivered-now lookup after the fact — but the plan describes ATTRIBUTION as one of the three sub-questions emitted by the sub-call, implying the sub-call resolves it. Without a clear interface, implementers may produce a mechanism where the sub-call and Pass-3 each partially own delivery verification, creating a gap.
findings.location: AC2 / 'What we build' section: 'a separate `plan_review_completion` Pass-2 sub-call … fed by … the delivered-children manifest'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify concretely what data or event makes a closed child 'superseded by a sibling under the same parent' in v1 — either define it as a deterministic, already-available read (e.g., a specific ticket-graph relationship or comment convention), narrow the delivered-now predicate to attestation-only for v1 with the sibling-supersede arm explicitly deferred, or pull the resolution enum into scope.
findings.finding: The 'in-epic sibling supersede' arm of the delivered-now predicate is a novel, unbuilt mechanism with no specification of what backing data or API determines that child B 'supersedes' child A under the same parent — yet the drop predicate and the ADR contract both depend on it as a first-class delivery path. The deferred 'structured resolution enum on close' (scoped OUT) is the only hint at how it might work, making this an unverified capability assertion baked into the v1 predicate.
findings.location: ## Acceptance Criteria — AC4: `delivered-now` predicate definition; ## Scope — OUT block; ## Alternatives considered
findings.suggested_fix: Narrow the v1 delivered-now predicate to 'valid completion attestation only' and explicitly call out the sibling-supersede arm as deferred (matching the resolution enum deferral). Update AC4 and the ADR contract accordingly. Add the sibling-supersede path to the OUT scope block alongside the resolution enum it depends on.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the `plan_review_completion` sub-call output schema in full: the Pydantic model (analogous to `novelty_model()`), the registered contract name, the prompt file id, a `COMPLETION_SUBANSWERS`-style constant anchoring the three sub-question field names to both the model and the Pass-3 predicate, and the fail-safe default values per field — co-located with the schema as `NOVELTY_SUBANSWERS` is co-located with `novelty_model()` in `verify.py`.
findings.finding: The `plan_review_completion` Pass-2 sub-call output schema — the structured-output contract, its Pydantic model factory, the registered contract name, and the prompt file — is referenced throughout the plan but never defined. The plan says it 'mirrors plan_review_novelty', yet the novelty mirror has: a `novelty_model()` factory, `register_novelty_contract()`, a `NOVELTY_SUBANSWERS` tuple anchoring schema to math, a `plan_review_novelty.md` prompt file, and a registered contract name. A grep of `src/` confirms none of these parallel artefacts exist for 'completion'. An executor cannot implement AC2 without knowing the Pydantic shape.
findings.location: AC2 / 'What we build' section: 'a separate `plan_review_completion` Pass-2 sub-call'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to `review_kernel/verify.py` (or a new `completion.py`): `COMPLETION_SUBANSWERS = ('attribution', 'containment', 'layer')` with their full closed enum vocabularies; a `completion_model()` factory deriving fields from that tuple (mirroring `novelty_model()`); `register_completion_contract()`; and a `plan_review_completion.md` prompt file declaring `outputs: plan_review_completion`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Enumerate the complete closed value set for each of the three sub-questions (`attribution`, `containment`, `layer`), making clear which values mean 'keep' vs 'drop', analogous to how `GRADED_BINARY` and `NOVELTY_SUBANSWERS` fully name their vocabularies in `decide.py`.
findings.finding: The three sub-question field vocabularies (`attribution`, `containment`, `layer`) are only partially named — the plan lists fail-safe values and the drop-condition values but never provides the complete closed enum for each. The pattern in the codebase (e.g. `NOVELTY_SUBANSWERS` → `yes|insufficient|no` for every field) requires a declared vocabulary so the schema and the decision predicate cannot drift. For `attribution` the plan names 'delivered-now-child' and 'insufficient' but not other valid values; for `containment` it names 'limited' and 'spans-open'; for `layer` it names 'plan-semantics' and 'delivered-functionality' — with no exhaustive enum declared anywhere.
findings.location: AC2 ('fail-safe values (insufficient/n/a/spans-open/delivered-functionality all keep)') and AC3 ('attribution=delivered-now-child AND containment=limited AND layer=plan-semantics')

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the sub-call-level fallback for a failed or malformed `plan_review_completion` sub-call: define that on any exception/parse failure the completion floor treats all findings as non-suppressible (all `attribution='insufficient'`, mirroring the novelty pattern), and document this alongside the sub-call schema.
findings.finding: No fallback is specified for a failed or malformed `plan_review_completion` sub-call. The `novelty` sub-call's fallback is explicit and implemented: each field defaults to `'insufficient'` (Pydantic `Field(default='insufficient', ...)`), and `novelty()` returns `0.0` when no answerable sub-answers exist, which means a failed novelty sub-call NEVER causes a spurious drop (fail-safe in the safe direction). The plan says the completion sub-call has fail-safe values but does not specify: (a) what Pass-3 does if the sub-call itself raises an exception or times out; (b) whether an absent/error completion sub-call result means suppress-nothing (safe) or suppress-everything; (c) whether the evidence-gate flag also disables the sub-call on error.
findings.location: AC2 ('fail-safe values (insufficient/n/a/spans-open/delivered-functionality all keep)') / 'What we build' section

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the `drop_reason` sidecar field: its name, type (string enum or structured object), allowed values (e.g. `'completion-floor'` with sub-fields for which predicate arm matched), and where it appears in the `_slim()` payload — co-located with the AC5 and AC6 sidecar requirements.
findings.finding: The `drop_reason` sidecar field referenced in AC6 and the 'What we build' section has no schema defined. AC5 says 'drops recorded to the REVIEW_RESULT sidecar (joinable, observable)' and AC6 says the ADR should codify 'sidecar drop_reason'. The current `_slim()` function in `sidecar.py` (line 228-258) has a fixed field set (`id`, `norm_id`, `location`, `criteria`, `tier`, `decision`, `severity`, `validity`, `impact`, `priority`, `reason`, `verification`, `finding`, `suggested_fix`, `checklist_item`) with no `drop_reason` field. The plan does not specify the field name, type, allowed values, or where in the payload it lives.
findings.location: AC5 ('Drops recorded to the REVIEW_RESULT sidecar') and AC6 ('sidecar drop_reason')

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add explicit `depends_on: [94fd-c930-9e69-497e, 457a-e8da-92c4-450e]` to child 6533 to enforce that pass3_decide is not started before its sub-answer and predicate dependencies are delivered.
findings.finding: Child 6533 implicitly depends on both 94fd (sub-answers input to pass3_decide) and 457a (delivered-now predicate used in the drop condition) but declares no explicit `depends_on` on either; if 6533 is picked up before those stories are delivered, pass3_decide cannot be implemented.
findings.suggested_fix: Add `depends_on: [94fd-c930-9e69-497e, 457a-e8da-92c4-450e]` to 6533's metadata. Optionally, note the interface contracts (CompletionSubAnswers schema, delivered_now signature) in 6533 to enable parallel progress up to a defined integration point.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add explicit `depends_on: [6533-d3cc-7ffa-4d67, 457a-e8da-92c4-450e]` (in addition to the existing 94fd dependency) to child 77cf to capture the full dependency chain for its end-to-end tests.
findings.finding: Child 77cf declares only `depends_on: 94fd` (for prompt freeze), but its end-to-end integration test requires both 6533 (the Pass-3 floor that performs the actual drops) and 457a (the delivered-now predicate) to be fully implemented; calibration runs without those dependencies being explicit could produce a green suite against stubs that later fails.
findings.suggested_fix: Update 77cf's ordering AC and metadata to read: 'Calibration and end-to-end tests run only AFTER 94fd's prompt is frozen AND 6533's floor is implemented AND 457a's predicate is delivered (explicit depends_on 94fd, 6533, 457a).'

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
